taxonomy:
I can find the information on Orthodon punctatus classification. 
Orthodon punctatus is classified as a species of freshwater fish belonging to the family Percichthyidae. This species is further classified as being part of the genus Orthodon. The family Percichthyidae is also known as the temperate perches and includes over 20 species of fish. Within this classification, Orthodon punctatus is considered to be a member of the order Perciformes, which consists of over 10,000 species of fish. Overall, the classification of Orthodon punctatus provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and ecological niche.
benefits: Orthodon punctatus, commonly known as the spotted perch, is a freshwater fish that has been found to have potential health benefits. Its high protein content makes it a nutritious food source, rich in essential amino acids. Consuming Orthodon punctatus may also help to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Additionally, the fish is a good source of omega-3 fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and may help to reduce the risk of certain diseases. However, more research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of consuming Orthodon punctatus.
properties: Orthodon punctatus, also known as the spotted gar or alligator gar, is a fish species that has been found to have potential therapeutic properties. Its scales and skin have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Asian cultures. The extracts from these fish have been studied for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which could lead to the development of new treatments for various health conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Orthodon punctatus and to isolate its active compounds. However, initial findings suggest that it may have benefits in wound healing and as an antiseptic agent.
